Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Customs Act (marine pleasure craft)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Customs Act (marine pleasure craft)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Customs Act to provide an exception to the requirement, under section 11 of that Act, for persons aboard a marine pleasure craft, to present themselves at a customs office upon arrival in Canada.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Section 11 of the Customs Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (3) do not apply to any person who enters Canadian waters, including the inland waters, aboard a marine pleasure craft that does not carry passengers who have paid for passage and that is used exclusively for pleasure while proceeding from one place outside Canada to another place outside Canada without anchoring or docking in Canada.
